title: Cambodia National Flag | History & Facts - Flagmakers
highlights:
The Khmer Rouge established control of Khmer Republic in 1975 renaming the country Democratic Kampuchea. A plain red flag with yellow three-towered monument similar to the Angkor Wat was adopted as the national flag. After the Vietnamese invasion of Cambodia 1979 the Salvation Front took power creating the People’s Republic of Kampuchea. The flag adopted was the plain red field with five towered m yellow monument similar to the Angkor Wat in the centre. The Flag of Democratic Kampuchea (1975 – 1979)

The Flag of the People’s Republic of Kampuchea (1979 – 1989) In 1989 the country became the State of Cambodia and the flag became a red-blue horizontal bicolour with a more ornate  ...    In 1993 the original blue-red-blue horizontal triband with white three-towered Angkor Wat at the centre was adopted. Each element of the flag has a specific meaning.
